authorization: Who is the executive sponsor who can approve scope, funding, and priority tradeoffs?
Who is the business owner accountable for outcomes after delivery?
Who is the project manager, delivery lead, or accountable execution owner?
What approval forum or individual authorizes initiation?
purpose: What approved business case or decision created the need for this project?
What business outcome does the project support?
Why does this need to happen now rather than later?
scope: What specific capabilities, processes, teams, systems, locations, or customer segments are in scope?
What is explicitly out of scope for this charter, even if related?
Which deliverables are authorized now, and which are only future candidates?
success: What measurable result will tell the sponsor this project worked?
Which operational, financial, customer, risk, or compliance measures matter most?
Which metrics lack a baseline and must be baselined during planning?
governance: What steering rhythm will be used?
What decisions can the project team make without sponsor approval?
What scope, budget, schedule, or risk changes require escalation?
What change-control model will prevent uncontrolled expansion?
